title: astragalus geyeri
intro: Astragalus geyeri, also known as Geyer's milkvetch, is a perennial plant native to the western United States. It belongs to the legume family and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory effects. The roots and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to boost the immune system, reduce fatigue, and alleviate stress and anxiety, offering potential health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and enhanced respiratory function. Astragalus geyeri can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in different ways.
